sections.paragraph:
θανατόω fut. θανατώσω; 1 aor. ἐθανάτωσα. Pass.: 1 fut. θανατωθήσομαι; 1 aor. ἐθανατώθην; pf. τεθανάτωμαι LXX (s. prec. entry; Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; LXX; TestSol 20:5; 22:20 P; Philo, Joseph., Test12Patr, Just.)

① to cause cessation of life, put to death lit. τινά kill someone, hand someone over to be killed, esp. of the death sentence and its execution (as X., An. 2, 6, 4; Pla., Leg. 9, p. 872c; Aelian, VH 5, 18; Ex 21:12ff; Sus 28; 1 Macc 1:57; 4 Macc 8:25) Mt 10:21; 26:59; 27:1; Mk 13:12; 14:55. The obj. acc. is easily supplied in θανατώσουσιν ἐξ ὑμῶν they will put some of you to death Lk 21:16. Pass. 2 Cor 6:9 (for the wordplay ἀποθνῄσκοντες … καὶ μὴ θανατούμενοι dying … but not under penalty of death cp. Ps.-Callisth. 1, 33, 11 p. 36, 21 θανὼν καὶ μὴ θανών); 1 Pt 3:18; 1 Cl 12:2; B 12:2; Dg 5:12. Be in danger of death Ro 8:36 (Ps 43:23.—Vi. Aesopi W 9 P. of ill treatment over a period of time: κατὰ πᾶσαν ἀποκτείνεις ἡμέραν).

② to cause total cessation of an activity, put to death, extirpate (Lycurgus 61 πολεώς ἐστι θάνατος ἀνάστατον=destruction spells a city’s death) fig. ext. of 1 τὶ someth. τὰς πράξεις τοῦ σώματος Ro 8:13.

③ to cause death that transcends the physical, bring death

ⓐ of spiritual or eternal death 1 Cl 39:7 (Job 5:2); Hs 9, 20, 4. θ. τινὰ ἔν τινι bring death to someone by someth. m 12, 1, 3; cp. 12, 2, 2.

ⓑ of the death that the believer dies through mystic unity w. the body of the crucified Christ; τῷ νόμῳ (dat. of disadvantage) Ro 7:4 (on rabb. associations s. WDiezinger, NovT 5, ’62, 268–98).—DELG s.v. θάνατος. EDNT. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

prec. prec. = preceding

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

esp. esp. = especially

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

obj. obj. = object, objective

acc. acc. = accusative

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Vi. Vi. Aesopi , = Vitae Aesopi, I a.d.—List 5

Lycurgus Lycurgus , IV b.c.—List 5

ext. ext. = extension

someth. someth. = something

Hs Hs = Similitudes

m m = Mandates

dat. dat. = dative

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

NovT NovT = Novum Testamentum (periodical)—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 443
